steps.title: Separate the second fraction
steps.content:
Rewrite the second equation so it uses the given fraction $\frac{x}{y}$.
$$\frac{cx}{5y}=\frac{c}{5}\cdot\frac{x}{y}$$

steps.title: Substitute the known ratio
steps.content:
Since $\frac{x}{y}=60$, replace that part of the expression.
$$\frac{c}{5}\cdot 60=60$$
Divide both sides by $60$.
$$\frac{c}{5}=1$$

steps.title: Solve for c
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $5$.
$$c=5$$
The answer is A.
